page: Amazon Web Services c6in.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the c6in.4xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In CPU-intensive tasks, it achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1891.58 and a multi-core score of 15444.96. The processor's support for AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions contributes to a PassMark CPU score of 21375.14 and an encryption test score of 14582.83. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 827.25 GB/sec, which drops to 91.31 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. For database operations, the instance handles 15.84 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a PassMark database score of 7805.48.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6in.4xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile tailored for network-bound and compute-heavy workloads without the overhead of local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage means all disk I/O depends on EBS performance, which is a key architectural tradeoff. While it performs adequately for small-scale language model inference, such as 239.68 tokens per second for a 135M model, its performance drops to a weak 31.96 tokens per second for 7B model prompt processing, indicating it is not suited for larger AI workloads. The instance is best fitted for compute-intensive applications, network-optimized workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating at 3.5 GHz, it delivers 16 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Benchmark data shows solid mid-range performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 21375.14 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.84 million operations per second. While highly capable for network-bound and compute-heavy tasks, its memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and lack of local NVMe storage represent key tradeoffs. It is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, network-optimized applications, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps bandwidth, powered by Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A compute-optimized, network-enhanced virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed network bandwidth for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Optimized for EBS and network-bound workloads with no local storage.
Delivers 15.84 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmark testing.
Best suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and software compilation.
